handoff_issues.description: PERSISTENT P1 (4th consecutive tinker report): digest-reviews.js processedWinIds fallback at lines 143-144 still present. However, IMPACT IS MITIGATED — the decider's direct-read fallback is successfully processing c2 reviews. Latest run processed 10 c2 reviews despite digest showing 0 pending. Only WIN-061.c2.json remains unprocessed (written today, will be picked up next run).
handoff_issues.evidence: digest-reviews.js lines 143-144 unchanged: `const isProcessed = processedSet.has(file) || processedSet.has(entry.win_id) || processedWinIds.has(entry.win_id)`. pending-digest.json: pending_count=0. Yet decider daily-report-2026-04-08T21-00.json shows curmudgeon_reviews_processed includes 10 c2 reviews. Pipeline IS functional via fallback but wastes tokens reading full review files instead of compact digest.

suggested_fix.rationale: 4th consecutive escalation. Impact lower than originally assessed (decider has working fallback) but the bug silently breaks the digest's intended purpose. Fix is safe — processedSet already has full filenames for both cycles.

handoff_issues.description: Dual processed-reviews lists creating drift: processed-reviews.json has both 'processed' (127 entries) and 'processed_reviews' (69 entries) with imperfect overlap. digest-reviews.js reads 'processed' only. EXHIBIT-AB.c2.json and WIN-060.c2.json are in 'processed_reviews' but NOT in 'processed', meaning they'll appear as unprocessed in the next digest generation despite already being handled.
handoff_issues.evidence: processed_reviews has 2 entries not in processed: EXHIBIT-AB.c2.json, WIN-060.c2.json. The 'processed' list has 60 entries not in 'processed_reviews'. Two lists tracking the same concept with different membership.

suggested_fix.replace:
// Read from both ledger lists and merge to handle decider's dual-list format
  const processedRaw = processedData ? [...new Set([...(processedData.processed || []), ...(processedData.processed_reviews || [])])] : [];
suggested_fix.rationale: Both lists track processed reviews. Merging them prevents re-processing reviews that were handled but only recorded in the secondary list. This is a defensive fix alongside the P1 bug fix.

prompt_issues.description: Decider prompt at 446 lines — 78% over the 250-line ceiling. GREW 32 lines since last report (was 414). This is the 4th consecutive report flagging this. LLM instruction-following degrades with prompt length; earlier sections get deprioritized.

suggested_fix.rationale: Not self-fixable — requires human refactor. Extract patch template schema (~50 lines), severity definitions (~30 lines), expansion integration procedure (~60 lines), and self-apply procedure (~80 lines) into monitor/prompts/decider-ref-*.md files loaded at runtime. Target: main prompt under 250 lines.

prompt_issues.description: Analyst prompt at 369 lines — 48% over ceiling. GREW 67 lines since last report (was 302). Growth is accelerating. Mode 3 globe fingerprint procedure (~80 lines) and V6 restructure context (~40 lines) should be extracted.

suggested_fix.rationale: Not self-fixable. Extract Mode 3 procedure into monitor/prompts/analyst-mode3.md and V6 context into monitor/prompts/v6-context.md. Both read at runtime only when needed.

prompt_issues.description: Curmudgeon prompt at 293 lines — 17% over ceiling. Manageable but trending upward.

suggested_fix.rationale: Lower priority. Monitor for further growth.

missing_patches.description: VERDICT CATEGORY OVERLOAD: 'Misleading' verdict conflates 4 distinct failure modes. Taxonomy restructuring requires editorial judgment.
